skill.name: code-reviewer
skill.description: Automated code review guidance with security checklists, quality review workflows, performance review references, and an optional local helper script for scanner reports.
skill.summary: Review code for security, quality, performance, testing, and maintainability issues.
skill.author: AutumnsGrove

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many alerts, but most are intentional vulnerable examples in markdown documentation for teaching reviewers what to find. The executable helper uses fixed local scanner commands and can write a report to a user-supplied path, so the remaining risk is operational and should be disclosed. No evidence found of prompt injection, credential exfiltration, obfuscation, or confirmed malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Local External Tool Execution
medium_findings.description: The helper script invokes local scanners with subprocess.run. This is a legitimate review feature, and the command arrays are fixed, but users should understand it executes installed tools against the selected path.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct subprocess.run usage is present in executable code. The risk is moderated because the commands are fixed scanner invocations and no shell execution was found.

medium_findings.title: User-Supplied Report Output Path
medium_findings.description: The helper script writes reports to the path provided through the output option. This is expected CLI behavior, but it can overwrite writable files if the operator chooses a sensitive destination.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write is confirmed in executable code and depends on a user-provided option. It is not hidden or automatic, so the impact is limited to normal local CLI permissions.

low_findings.title: Educational Vulnerability Examples Triggered Static Rules
low_findings.description: Many high and critical static alerts are examples inside a security training guide. They are labeled as vulnerable patterns or secure alternatives and are not executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged snippets appear in markdown sections that demonstrate unsafe and safe code patterns for reviewers. This strongly supports a false positive classification for those static alerts.

low_findings.title: Documentation Command Snippets Are Not Auto-Executed
low_findings.description: Static analysis matched shell-style examples and checklist text in documentation. These snippets tell users how to run review tools and do not execute during skill loading.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are markdown documentation or checklist entries, including an npm configuration reminder. No automatic execution path or embedded credential was found at these locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: subprocess.run Calls for Local Review Tools
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper script launches Bandit, Radon, Pylint, and Safety as local subprocesses. The pattern is operationally sensitive but aligned with the advertised code review purpose.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess pattern is confirmed in executable code. The commands are fixed and tool-specific, which lowers confidence that this is an exploitable security issue.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local File Write for Generated Reports
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper script writes generated review output to a caller-selected file path. Users should run it in trusted workspaces and avoid privileged output locations.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write is present in executable code and uses local process permissions. It is a normal report feature, not stealthy persistence or data exfiltration.

content.user_title: Review Code for Security and Quality
content.value_statement: Code reviews can miss security, quality, and performance issues when teams lack a consistent process. This skill provides structured review workflows, checklists, examples, and local helper tooling.
actual_capabilities: Guides security reviews for injection, authentication, authorization, data exposure, and cryptography issues.
Provides code quality review criteria for structure, complexity, naming, errors, and maintainability.
Includes performance review guidance for algorithms, database access, memory, network, caching, and concurrency.
Offers a checklist for security, testing, documentation, and language-specific review coverage.
Includes a Python helper that can run Bandit, Radon, Pylint, and Safety when those tools are installed.
Generates summary, markdown, or JSON-style review reports from local scanner results.
limitations: The helper script depends on external tools that must be installed separately.
Scanner output quality depends on the language, project setup, and available dependencies.
The skill provides review guidance and does not replace expert security review for high-risk systems.
The helper script runs local commands and should be used only in trusted workspaces.

use_cases.title: Review a Pull Request
use_cases.description: Apply a structured checklist to find security, quality, performance, testing, and documentation issues before merge.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Security Review
use_cases.description: Use the vulnerability examples and detection tips to focus review attention on common attack paths.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Review Practices
use_cases.description: Create repeatable review expectations for teams that need consistent feedback across languages and projects.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Pull Request Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small change
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this pull request for correctness, security, test coverage, and maintainability. List findings by severity and include file references when possible.

prompt_templates.title: Security-Focused Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused review for risky code paths
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this code for injection, authentication, authorization, data exposure, unsafe deserialization, and weak cryptography. Explain why each issue matters.

prompt_templates.title: Performance and Quality Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Review a service or module for production readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this module for complexity, resource usage, database efficiency, caching, concurrency risks, and maintainability. Prioritize changes with measurable impact.

prompt_templates.title: Release Readiness Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a release readiness review of these changes. Cover security, reliability, performance, testing, documentation, migration risks, and rollback concerns.

output_examples.input: A pull request adds a new user profile endpoint and updates database queries.
output: High: The endpoint needs authorization checks before returning profile data.
Medium: Add tests for missing users, private profiles, and invalid identifiers.
Low: Document the response fields in the API reference.

output_examples.input: A service change introduces new caching and background processing.
output: Medium: Define cache invalidation rules for update and delete operations.
Medium: Add timeout and retry limits for background jobs.
Low: Include metrics for queue length and job failure rates.

output_examples.input: A team wants a repeatable checklist for reviewing security-sensitive changes.
output: Start with authentication, authorization, input validation, and data exposure checks.
Run dependency and static analysis tools before human review.
Record unresolved risks with owners and deadlines.

best_practices: Run automated scanners before manual review so reviewers can focus on design, logic, and risk.
Prioritize findings by exploitability, user impact, and likelihood instead of listing every style issue first.
Include specific file references and actionable remediation steps in review feedback.
anti_patterns: Do not treat scanner output as complete proof that code is safe.
Do not run the helper script on untrusted repositories with privileged local permissions.
Do not mix minor style preferences with blocking security or correctness findings.

faq.question: Does this skill run code automatically?
faq.answer: No. The documentation guides reviews. The helper script runs only when the user invokes it locally.

faq.question: Which tools does the helper script use?
faq.answer: It can call Bandit, Radon, Pylint, and Safety when those tools are installed in the local environment.

faq.question: Can it replace a human security review?
faq.answer: No. It helps structure reviews and surface common issues, but high-risk systems still need expert review.

faq.question: Is it useful for non-Python projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The review guidance covers general security, quality, performance, testing, and documentation topics across languages.

faq.question: Why is the marketplace risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill includes a local helper script that launches external scanner commands and writes report files when requested.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report identifies support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
